best_for: long-form articles, magazine layouts, premium blogs
notes: High-stroke-contrast display headlines sit above a neutral grotesque body; keep headline tracking tight and let body line-height breathe.

best_for: fashion brands, boutique sites, premium product pages
notes: Delicate thin-weight display only sings at large sizes, so pair it with a humanist sans and lean on whitespace rather than weight for hierarchy.
Two serifs coexist because the display carries more personality while the body stays even-color; nudge the display heavier to separate the roles.

best_for: dev tools, API docs, engineering dashboards
notes: Reserve the mono for code, labels, and data so the geometric sans carries prose; their shared squared geometry keeps the two families related.

best_for: SaaS apps, onboarding flows, general marketing
notes: A slightly rounded display sans warms the headline while a workhorse body keeps screens legible; both hold up well at small interface sizes.

best_for: portfolios, art sites, bold landing pages
notes: Tightly tracked heavy Archivo headers feel architectural; the mono body adds intentional rawness, so keep the palette flat and the grid exposed.
An old-style serif at display size carries quiet authority while a calm sans handles UI; bump body size up a notch since Garamond runs small.
Two geometric sans share a clean spine, so build contrast through weight and size jumps rather than family; save heavy weights for short headlines.

best_for: health apps, wellness brands, government services
notes: Rounded terminals on the display read gentle and non-clinical; Public Sans keeps accessibility strong, so use high contrast and generous spacing.

best_for: creative agencies, food brands, feature stories
notes: Fraunces optical sizing lets headlines turn wonky and warm at large sizes; a neutral franklin body grounds it so personality stays in the titles.
Drawn as one family, the sans and mono share metrics and tone for a coherent engineered feel; use the mono for literal code and keystrokes.

best_for: consumer apps, community sites, lifestyle brands
notes: Bricolage mixes condensed and wide forms for a lively headline; balance that energy with a soft even body and avoid over-tracking the display.
Condensed Archivo headers pack confidence into tight columns; Inter handles dense numeric tables well, so switch on tabular figures for alignment.
Spectral is built for screen text yet has display presence; pair it with a strong sans for decks and captions to set a clear editorial rhythm.
One neutral family across a wide weight range proves restraint; build hierarchy purely with size, weight, and space, never a second face.
Oversized Bricolage headlines land instant impact; let DM Sans recede so the display does the talking, and keep line lengths short for punch.
The two serifs differ enough in contrast and width to signal heading versus body; hold the measure near 65 characters for comfortable reading.
Geist and its mono are tuned together for crisp UI; use the mono for metrics and shortcuts and rely on tight consistent spacing for polish.
Instrument Serif's single weight wants to be huge and sparse; let one large headline anchor the page and let Inter carry everything functional.
Designed as siblings, these share proportions so the pairing feels deliberate and quiet; ideal when credibility matters more than flair.
Crimson Pro's book warmth suits running headers and body alike, but a sans for navigation keeps interface elements from feeling dated.
Space Grotesk's quirky details give headlines character while Inter stays invisible in body; a dependable contemporary default for software.
Both families are engineered for legibility and letter disambiguation; combine them for low-vision audiences and keep contrast well above minimums.
Work Sans softens at display size while Manrope reads cleanly in body; the humanist warmth feels personal without losing interface structure.
A restrained serif headline signals heritage and trust; Inter with tabular figures handles dense financial data, balancing prestige and utility.
Sharing the Plex DNA, the serif and sans pair effortlessly; use the serif for headlines and pull quotes to warm an otherwise systematic feel.
Two clean geometric sans keep the page quiet; favor thin display weights and large size jumps, and let whitespace carry most of the hierarchy.
Lora's moderate contrast reads gently at headline size; pairing with Inter yields a familiar comfortable structure that works across most content.
Archivo's wide range includes punchy condensed cuts for stacked headlines; Manrope keeps the body open so the density lives in the titles.
Fraunces brings playful warmth to headlines while Newsreader keeps body inviting; both have optical sizing, so set each at its intended size.
Space Mono's typewriter quirk adds a distinctive accent for labels and figures; keep it sparse against neutral Inter or it overwhelms the layout.
